query: Our retail-demand forecasting model has been in prod for 18 months. The PSI on weekly_units_sold just spiked from its usual 0.05 to 0.31 — well above our 0.25 'major' alert threshold. The alert is firing. The current window is mid-November and the reference window is from April. Is this real drift? Should we retrain?
expected_behavior: Identifies seasonality as a likely confound — November (holiday season) versus April (non-holiday) is an expected baseline shift for retail demand, not a signal of model degradation
Recommends recalibrating the noise floor using same-season or same-period-prior-year subwindows rather than adjacent-period subwindows for this seasonal feature
Does NOT recommend retraining or rolling back the model as the immediate response; advises ruling out seasonality first and possibly suppressing or re-baselining the alert
